blocks.content: Vincent woke up with a frown.
Wah. Wah.
blocks.speaker: Shirone

blocks.text: In the silent mountain where even birds were asleep, the crying of a newborn baby could be heard.

blocks.content: Wah. Wah.
blocks.speaker: Shirone

blocks.content: The baby's sorrowful cries continued as he shook his disheveled head and rubbed his sleepy eyes.
God, what have I done so wrong?
Coming down from the bed, Vincent looked back at his wife who was in deep sleep.
He hoped she was having a happy dream.
If she heard this sound, she would surely have a hard time.
The Vincent couple, married for 7 years, had no children.
They had even visited a doctor spending a lot of money, but all they were told was that the cause was unknown.

blocks.speaker: Doctor
blocks.content: There's something called compatibility. It's just one of those things. Neither Olina nor you seem to have any particular problem, so try often, many times. Haha!
Vincent also laughed heartily at first.
It was true that their relationship had improved thanks to the doctor's prescription.
But as they entered their 5th year of marriage, he had to admit it.
He couldn't have children.
His wife, Olina, never expressed any resentment.
But sometimes when she looked at other people's children with a lonely expression, Vincent couldn't help but resent his own manhood.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Which bastard is this, not just rubbing salt in my wounds!
Due to the nature of his job as a hunter, he mostly lived in a house in the mountains.
This was necessary because he had to check traps regularly and needed a base camp in the mountains to track wild animals for days.
No one would visit a hunter's house at this hour.
Vincent, who couldn't rule out the possibility of thieves, grabbed a single-edged axe and went out the door.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Who's there! Who's making noise in the middle of the night!
Vincent shouted loud enough to make the mountains echo.
No one answered, and only his shout returned as an echo.
It could be a merchant dealing with specialty products, but no matter how much he looked around, not even a common torch was visible.
Vincent, becoming more tense, firmly gripped the axe handle.
Then he slowly moved, following the baby's cry.
The sound was coming from the stable.
It was highly likely to be thieves.
He had heard from the slash-and-burn farmers' village that robbers were rampant, targeting people living alone in the mountains.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Damn you! I'll cut you to pieces!
In the worst case, blood would have to be spilled.
With such resolution, Vincent flung open the stable door.
At the same time, he surveyed the inside with a hunter's excellent senses.
Purr.
Hearing the peaceful snorting of horses, he felt somewhat calmed.
This was because animals do not lie.
The stable, where two horses were sleeping, was small and cozy.
As such, there was nowhere to hide.
But surprisingly, there was no sign that anyone had entered.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Then how could the crying sound...?
Vincent carefully moved his steps and examined the area covered with straw.
A newborn baby, about two months old, was crying.
He hid the axe behind his back, feeling an inexplicable sense of holiness.
Then, thinking even this was irreverent, he threw it to the corner and crawled toward the baby.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Wah. Wah.
When he opened the swaddling cloth with his rough hands, there was a baby as beautiful as the moon.
An innocent baby who knew nothing and had just encountered the air of the world.
Vincent's pupils trembled.
Then, as if struck by lightning, he shook his body and, unable to control his emotions, darted out of the stable.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Who's there! Who's playing tricks! Abandoning a child, you rotten bastard! Come out now!
The echo resounded as if several people were speaking.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Come out! Are you really not coming out? How could you abandon a child! You're truly a bad person! You know that?
He urged once more, but there was no answer.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: You really abandoned it! No more chances! Just try appearing before me! I'll make your face into rice cake!
Vincent shouted with the loudest voice of his life.
He didn't want to leave any regrets when reminiscing about today in the distant future.
Vincent entered the stable again.
The baby had fallen asleep, exhausted from crying.
With trembling hands, he embraced the baby and gently placed his ear against the small chest.
The heart was beating.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Honey, what's going on?
His wife, Olina, entered the stable, gasping for breath.
Vincent's voice had been loud enough to completely wake her from sleep.
Vincent, at a loss for words, stared blankly at his wife before showing her the baby sleeping in his arms.

blocks.speaker: Olina
blocks.content: What's that baby?

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: It seems... it's our child.
In the early summer, when blue was rampant.
Vincent hurried his steps, humming a tune while following the path of cold flowing stream.
A large roe deer was hanging over his shoulder, but the movements of his large body were only light.
As home drew near, his steps quickened.
The faces of his precious family were already before his eyes.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone, Dad's home!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Dad!
A 12-year-old boy ran to the entrance with a broad smile.
Unlike Vincent, who was like a rugged rock, Shirone had grown into a beautiful boy, as if specially cherished by God.
With his golden hair flowing like golden thread, blue eyes, and a straight nose bridge even though not fully grown, he looked like a doll.
Every time he saw his son like this, Vincent couldn't help but feel proud and stand tall.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: So, my son. My treasure. Have you been well?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes. I helped Mom with cooking and read many books.
Cooking and books.
Feeling the dissonance between these two words, Vincent was perplexed.
But he didn't show it and burst into hearty laughter.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Do you like books that much?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: No, well... there's not much else to do.
Seeing his son hiding his emotions as if he had done something wrong, Vincent felt sorry and guilty.
In fact, he knew.
That this miraculous child given by heaven was extraordinarily intelligent.
Though Olina knew a little about writing, she was still a mountain hunter's wife.
Nevertheless, Shirone had stumbled through learning letters with just what he had been taught, and now he had reached a level where he could read books on his own.
That's why it hurt even more.
A hunter's child couldn't study.
A herbalist's child becomes a herbalist, a hunter's child becomes a hunter—that was the only way to make a living.
No matter how humble a profession, there are secrets and knacks that cannot be passed on by word alone.
The only thing that poor Vincent could pass on to his son was hunting skills.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: No, you did well. Whatever you do, you need to learn to succeed. This time when Dad goes to the city, I'll buy you a book.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It's okay. You've already bought me many. And honestly, they didn't have much content.
Vincent laughed at his son's bluff.
Practical books that could make money were too expensive to even consider, and he had bought discarded books from nobles at bargain prices from antique shops.
As far as he knew, they weren't books at a level that a child could digest.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Alright! By the way, how about it? Want to go chop wood with Dad? Learning is important, but a man needs physical strength. Today, I'll teach you how to chop wood.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Wow! So you're giving me an axe too?
Vincent gave Shirone an axe as if he had been waiting for this moment.
Though it was expensive compared to their living, it was much more useful than books.
An axe could help earn money.
Honestly, he wanted his son to become a mountain hunter.
But Shirone was small for his age and didn't seem capable of doing such hard work.
So he thought of building his strength from now on.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: His face has noble features and his mind is exceptionally good. Could he have been a noble's child?
Vincent hastily erased this doubt from his mind.
Every time he had such thoughts, he felt overwhelmed as if receiving an undeserved gift, only to be followed by guilt.
Shirone is my child. Not a child taken from a stable, but my child with my blood.
With such determination, Vincent took Shirone's hand and left the house.
His logging area was about 1 kilometer away from the hut.
Because there were many mountain hunters nearby, fights could break out if one worked outside the designated area.
Sometimes it even led to knife fights.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Now. I'll demonstrate, so watch carefully and follow.
Vincent spit on his palm and then started chopping the tree with a refreshing motion.
Not long after the thudding sound started, the straight tree fell with a crackling noise.
The number of axe strokes was a measure of a lumberjack's skill.
Vincent was not a lumberjack, so he toppled it with 10 strokes, but even that was an impressive skill.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: This way, you chop the same spot several times, and when the tree leans, it collapses unable to bear its weight. Can you do it?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, I'll try.
Vincent chose a tree that Shirone could chop.
Standing in front of the tree, Shirone spit on his hands just as his father had done.
Though he had only seen it once, his positioning, posture, and even the habit of rubbing his palms were identical to his father's.
Vincent smiled happily, thinking indeed, he was smart.
But when he actually tried to swing the axe, his posture was somehow flimsy.
It's not something that can be done with just the head. Strength and physical condition must support it.
The axe was quite heavy, and strong muscles were essential to swing it enough to cut through the wind.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: The reason Vincent had brought Shirone to chop wood was that his build was smaller compared to his peers.
If he couldn't become a scholar, he should at least build strength.
No woman would want to marry a man who couldn't earn money.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Heek! Eek!
Shirone gritted his teeth and swung the axe.
But each swing hit a different spot.
Unable to watch any longer, Vincent gave him some tips.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Don't use all your strength. Instead of that, reduce your power a bit but increase your accuracy.
Shirone quickly absorbed Vincent's advice.
But this time, his strength was too weak, showing no signs of breaking the tree.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Was my son always this weak?
Vincent became a bit downcast.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Phew, this is hard.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: It's okay, Shirone. No, I'm sorry. To be honest, I know that this kind of work doesn't suit you. But the child of a hunter, inevitably...

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: You're a really intelligent child. More than Baron, the herbalist's son, more than Stella, the fruit seller's daughter. You don't need to feel inferior because you lack strength. My greed is...
Tears welled up in Vincent's eyes due to his sorrowful feelings.
But Shirone paid no attention to that and pulled his father's collar, asking:

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: More importantly, Dad, how can I chop the tree well?
Vincent felt a bit embarrassed.
But the embarrassment was brief, and when Shirone showed interest in wood chopping, a sense of elation swept over him.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Do you really want to learn this?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Yes, teach me. It's fun.
His son's words gave him courage again.
Vincent, reinvigorated, took Shirone and examined the notch in the tree closely.
Now, look at this notch.
Strength will come naturally when you grow up.
But in fact, this isn't a job that requires a lot of strength.
What's important is technique.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: I told you earlier to chop the same spot, but if you twist the angle just a little, it will receive much more force.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Ah, I see.
Vincent squinted his eyes and stared intently at the notch Shirone had made.
It was quite surprising.
He had hit the exact same spot each time, which was hard to believe for a beginner.
At this rate, it would actually be difficult to fell the tree unless one had exceptional strength.
